obligations.reason: The adbg_axi_module translates JTAG debug commands to AXI bus transactions. Without a permission guard, an external debugger could read/write any memory/peripheral, bypassing CPU privilege protections.
obligations.subject: JTAG debug interface (adbg_tap_top)
obligations.object: system memory and peripherals on AXI bus
obligations.expected_guard: debug enable state or authentication signal that gates AXI transaction generation
obligations.candidate_path: adbg_tap_top -> adbg_top -> adbg_axi_module -> adbg_axi_biu -> AXI master
obligations.uncertainty: Whether debug enable is implemented and its default value needs confirmation.

obligations.reason: The adbg_lint_module provides a LINT bus interface for debug. Without a permission check, an attacker could access TCDM memory or peripherals, potentially reading secrets or modifying configuration.
obligations.subject: JTAG debug interface
obligations.expected_guard: debug enable or authentication signal that gates LINT transaction generation
obligations.candidate_path: adbg_tap_top -> adbg_top -> adbg_lint_module -> adbg_lint_biu -> LINT bus
obligations.uncertainty: Is there a global debug enable that covers both AXI and LINT? Need to trace the inhibit or enable signal.

obligations.reason: The adbg_top module selects which debug module (AXI, LINT, etc.) is active based on TAP command. If module_select_i is not gated by a security state, a debugger could activate a debug module that accesses sensitive resources without authorization.
obligations.subject: JTAG TAP controller (adbg_tap_top)
obligations.object: debug module activation (module_select_i and top_inhibit_o signals)
obligations.expected_guard: a lock or permission register that restricts which modules can be selected; or authenticated state required before able to select
obligations.uncertainty: May not be a direct permission flaw if module selection is benign, but cross-module inhibition could impact security.

obligations.reason: The adbg_or1k_status_reg contains debug control states (e.g., stop, reset, debug enable). If these bits are writable without a lock or one-time write protection, a debugger could disable debug locks or enable debug after security has locked it down.
obligations.subject: JTAG debug interface
obligations.expected_guard: sticky lock bit that, once set, prevents further writes to critical debug enable bits; or authenticated-only access
obligations.candidate_path: on-chip debug logic -> adbg_or1k_status_reg register file
state_conditions_to_check: how lock bit gates writes to other status bits
obligations.uncertainty: Need to verify if the status register actually contains security-critical bits and if a lock is implemented.

obligations.reason: If after reset the debug interface is enabled by default, an attacker could use JTAG before any software lock-down, allowing early-stage compromise. Expected that debug is disabled until explicitly enabled after secure boot.
obligations.subject: JTAG debug interface (adbg_tap_top)
obligations.expected_guard: reset default of debug enable = 0 (disabled); or requires authenticated sequence to enable
obligations.candidate_path: reset signal (trstn_i) -> debug enable register -> gate debug paths
state_conditions_to_check: reset value of any debug_enable register
obligations.uncertainty: The SoC might have a separate secure boot that disables debug later; but hardware default should be safe.

obligations.reason: The debug LINT bus connects to both soc_domain and safe_domain. A debug-initiated transaction should not access safe_domain resources unless explicitly authorized, else it bypasses domain isolation.
obligations.subject: Debug LINT master (adbg_lint_module)
obligations.expected_guard: address range checker or security attribute that blocks debug transactions targeting safe_domain
state_conditions_to_check: debug attribute on bus can bypass isolation
obligations.uncertainty: Need to verify if safe_domain has any bus-level firewall; otherwise this is a valid concern.

evidence_slots.subject: JTAG debug interface (adbg_tap_top)
evidence_slots.object: system memory and peripherals on AXI bus
evidence_slots.expected_guard: debug enable state or authentication signal that gates AXI transaction generation
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: adbg_axi_module accepts JTAG commands and initiates AXI transactions unconditionally when selected; no debug_enable or authentication input is present. The module's FSM transitions directly from idle to burst operations upon command decode, without checking any permission signal.
evidence_slots.path: adbg_tap_top -> adbg_top -> adbg_axi_module -> adbg_axi_biu -> AXI master
evidence_slots.state_condition: After reset, module_id_reg is 0, selecting the AXI module. Once a burst read/write command is received, the state machine starts AXI transactions without any enable check.
evidence_slots.impact: An external debugger can read/write any memory or peripheral on the AXI bus, completely bypassing CPU privilege protections and secure boot.

rtl_evidence.description: module_selects[0] connected directly to adbg_axi_module module_select_i without gating.
Command decoding uses operation_in without any security check; no enable signal observed.
State machine transitions from STATE_Rstatus to STATE_Rburst to start read, and from STATE_Wwait to STATE_Wburst for writes, both without a permission condition.
No debug enable define present in the global defines file.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Missing debug enable guard: AXI debug module generates bus transactions without any authentication or permission check. This allows unrestricted debug access to all memory and peripherals.

evidence_slots.subject: JTAG debug interface
evidence_slots.expected_guard: debug enable or authentication signal that gates LINT transaction generation
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: adbg_lint_module decodes JTAG commands and initiates LINT bus transactions without any permission signal. The module's state machine starts burst reads/writes immediately upon command, and no debug_enable input exists in the port list.
evidence_slots.path: adbg_tap_top -> adbg_top -> adbg_lint_module -> adbg_lint_biu -> LINT bus
evidence_slots.state_condition: After reset, the LINT module is idle but ready; when selected via JTAG, a burst command triggers the state machine to start LINT transactions without checking any enable bit.
evidence_slots.impact: An attacker can access internal TCDM memory and peripherals via JTAG, reading secrets or modifying configuration.

rtl_evidence.description: Command decoding and operation latch without any guard.
Next-state logic for burst reads/writes transitions solely on command and TAP state, no permission check.
Module port list shows no debug_enable or authenticated input.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Missing debug enable guard for LINT debug module: JTAG-initiated LINT transactions can proceed without any authentication, exposing internal bus resources.

evidence_slots.subject: JTAG TAP controller (adbg_tap_top)
evidence_slots.object: debug module activation (module_select_i and top_inhibit_o signals)
evidence_slots.expected_guard: a lock or permission register that restricts which modules can be selected; or authenticated state required before able to select
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Module selection is performed by writing to the input shift register with a select command, and the module_id_reg is updated directly on update_dr_i if !select_inhibit. There is no lock bit or authentication required to change the selected module. The select_inhibit signal only prevents switching during ongoing burst transactions, not for security.
evidence_slots.state_condition: When debug_select_i is asserted and a select command is present, module_id_reg is updated unconditionally (after reset it is 0, selecting AXI). No security state gates this update.
evidence_slots.impact: An attacker can switch the active debug module to any supported module, including ones with access to sensitive resources, without any authorization.

rtl_evidence.description: module_id_reg update logic: condition debug_select_i && select_cmd && update_dr_i && !select_inhibit. No lock or permission bit is checked.
module_selects are derived directly from module_id_reg; no additional gating.
Defines for module IDs (WISHBONE, CPU0, CPU1, JSP) but no lock configuration.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Missing module selection lock: No authentication or sticky lock prevents an attacker from switching the active debug module via JTAG, allowing access to any debug interface.

evidence_slots.subject: JTAG debug interface
evidence_slots.expected_guard: sticky lock bit that, once set, prevents further writes to critical debug enable bits; or authenticated-only access
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: The adbg_or1k_status_reg module has a write enable (we_i) input; data_i is written directly to internal control registers (ctrl_reg_o). No lock bit or write-once mechanism was found. The register can be rewritten at any time via debug commands.
evidence_slots.path: on-chip debug logic -> adbg_or1k_status_reg register file
evidence_slots.state_condition: When we_i is asserted (driven by OR1K debug module), the control register is updated. No lock condition gates these writes.
evidence_slots.impact: Debug control bits (e.g., stop, reset, debug enable) can be modified by an attacker at will, potentially disabling security locks or re-enabling debug after lock-down.

rtl_evidence.description: Module port list and inferred logic: we_i directly enables register update; no lock bit present.
Defines file includes no lock bit define.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: File content truncated; full register write logic and lock implementation could not be confirmed from available snippet, but no lock signal was observed in the port list or logic.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Missing lock on debug status register: writes to critical debug control bits are unprotected, allowing unauthorized modification.
inspection_records.notes: Further, unobserved logic may contain a lock; based on available evidence, no guard found.

evidence_slots.subject: JTAG debug interface (adbg_tap_top)
evidence_slots.expected_guard: reset default of debug enable = 0 (disabled); or requires authenticated sequence to enable
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: No debug enable register or global enable signal was found in the debug path. After reset, the adbg_top module initializes module_id_reg to 0, selecting the AXI module, and all debug sub-modules are ready to process JTAG commands immediately. There is no hardware default that keeps debug disabled at reset.
evidence_slots.path: reset signal (trstn_i) -> debug enable register -> gate debug paths
evidence_slots.state_condition: On trstn_i negedge, module_id_reg <= 0, which selects the AXI module. No other state disables debug operation; debug modules are fully operational once clock and reset are stable.
evidence_slots.impact: An attacker can access the debug interface immediately after reset, before any secure boot software has a chance to lock down debug, enabling early-stage compromise.

rtl_evidence.description: Reset of module_id_reg to 0, enabling AXI module.
Port list of adbg_top: no debug_enable or lock input.
Top-level debug interface wrapper shows no debug enable input from SoC.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Debug enable not default-disabled at reset: debug modules are active immediately after reset, allowing JTAG access before secure boot.

evidence_slots.subject: Debug LINT master (adbg_lint_module)
evidence_slots.expected_guard: address range checker or security attribute that blocks debug transactions targeting safe_domain
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: The LINT debug bus (lint_jtag_wrap) connects the debug module directly to the system LINT interconnect without any address filtering or firewall. No bus-level security attribute or domain crossing gate was found in the path to safe_domain.
evidence_slots.state_condition: Once a debug burst command is issued, the LINT master places an address on the bus. No check is performed to restrict addresses to soc_domain only; safe_domain regions are accessible if physically connected.
evidence_slots.impact: A debugger can access safe_domain memory and peripherals, breaking domain isolation and potentially compromising security-critical functions.

rtl_evidence.description: Direct connection from adbg_lintonly_top to XBAR_TCDM_Bus, no firewall instantiation.
s_lint_jtag_bus connected to interconnect; no domain isolation logic visible.
safe_domain module no observed debug access control input; bus connection appears unfiltered.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Full safe_domain bus firewall logic not inspected; but based on the lint_jtag_wrap and pulp_soc connectivity, no address-range check is apparent.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: No bus firewall for debug LINT transactions to safe_domain; debug-initiated accesses can reach safe resources, violating domain isolation.

155:    ///////////////////////////////////////
156:    // Combinatorial assignments
157: 
158:     assign select_cmd   = input_shift_reg[`DBG_TOP_MODULE_DATA_LEN-1];
159:     assign module_id_in = input_shift_reg[`DBG_TOP_MODULE_DATA_LEN-2:`DBG_TOP_MODULE_DATA_LEN-6];
160: 
161: //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
162: // Module select register and select signals
163: //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
164: 
165:     always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
166:     begin
167:         if (~trstn_i)
168:             module_id_reg <= 5'h0;
169:         else if(debug_select_i && select_cmd && update_dr_i && !select_inhibit)       // Chain select
170:             module_id_reg <= module_id_in;
171:     end
172: 
173:     always_comb
174:     begin
175:   		if ( module_id_reg == 0 )
176:             module_selects = 2'b01;
177:         else
178:             module_selects = 2'b10;
179:     end
180: //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
181: 
182: 
183: ///////////////////////////////////////////////
184: // Data input shift register
185: ///////////////////////////////////////////////
186: 
187: always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
188: begin
189:   if (~trstn_i)
190:     input_shift_reg <= 'h0;
191:   else if(debug_select_i && shift_dr_i)
192:     input_shift_reg <= {tdi_i, input_shift_reg[`DBG_TOP_MODULE_DATA_LEN-1:1]};
193: end

216:

40: // CVS Revision History
41: //
42: // $Log: adbg_wb_module.v,v $
43: // Revision 1.5  2010-01-13 00:55:45  Nathan
44: // Created hi-speed mode for burst reads.  This will probably be most beneficial to the OR1K module, as GDB does a burst read of all the GPRs each time a microinstruction is single-stepped.
45: //
46: // Revision 1.2  2009/05/17 20:54:57  Nathan
47: // Changed email address to opencores.org
48: //
49: // Revision 1.1  2008/07/22 20:28:33  Nathan
50: // Changed names of all files and modules (prefixed an a, for advanced).  Cleanup, indenting.  No functional changes.
51: //
52: // Revision 1.12  2008/07/11 08:13:30  Nathan
53: // Latch opcode on posedge, like other signals.  This fixes a problem when 
54: // the module is used with a Xilinx BSCAN TAP.  Added signals to allow modules 
55: // to inhibit latching of a new active module by the top module.  This allows 
56: // the sub-modules to force the top level module to ignore the command present
57: // in the input shift register after e.g. a burst read.
58: //

{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "220:    // Combinatorial assignments
221: 
222:    assign     module_cmd      = ~(data_register_i[63]);
223:    assign     operation_in    =   data_register_i[62:59];
224:    assign     address_data_in =   data_register_i[58:27];
225:    assign     count_data_in   =   data_register_i[26:11];
226: 
227:    assign     data_to_biu = {tdi_i,data_register_i[63:1]};
228: 
229:    assign     reg_select_data = data_register_i[58:57];
230: 
231:    ////////////////////////////////////////////////
232:           // Operation decoder
233: 
234:    // These are only used before the operation is latched, so decode them from operation_in
235:    assign intreg_instruction = ((operation_in == `DBG_AXI_CMD_IREG_WR) | (operation_in == `DBG_AXI_CMD_IREG_SEL));
236: 
237:    assign intreg_write = (operation_in == `DBG_AXI_CMD_IREG_WR);
238: 
239:    assign burst_write =  (operation_in == `DBG_AXI_CMD_BWRITE8)  | 
240:                          (operation_in == `DBG_AXI_CMD_BWRITE16) | 
241:                          (operation_in == `DBG_AXI_CMD_BWRITE32) | 
242:                          (operation_in == `DBG_AXI_CMD_BWRITE64); 
243: 
244:    assign burst_read  =  (operation_in == `DBG_AXI_CMD_BREAD8)  | 
245:                          (operation_in == `DBG_AXI_CMD_BREAD16) | 
246:                          (operation_in == `DBG_AXI_CMD_BREAD32) | 
247:                          (operation_in == `DBG_AXI_CMD_BREAD64); 
248: 
249:    // This is decoded from the registered operation
250:    always_comb
251:    begin
252:         case(operation)
253: 
254:        `DBG_AXI_CMD_BWRITE8:
255:         begin
256:            word_size_bits  = 6'd7;  // Bits is actually bits-1, to make the FSM easier
257:            word_size_bytes = 4'd1;
258:            rd_op           = 1'b0;
259:         end
260: 
261:        `DBG_AXI_CMD_BWRITE16:
262:         begin
263:            word_size_bits  = 6'd15;  // Bits is actually bits-1, to make the FSM easier
264:            word_size_bytes = 4'd2;
265:            rd_op           = 1'b0;
266:         end
267: 
268:        `DBG_AXI_CMD_BWRITE32:
269:         begin
270:            word_size_bits  = 6'd31;  // Bits is actually bits-1, to make the FSM easier
271:            word_size_bytes = 4'd4;
272:            rd_op           = 1'b0;
273:         end
274: 
275:        `DBG_AXI_CMD_BWRITE64:
276:         begin
277:            word_size_bits  = 6'd63;  // Bits is actually bits-1, to make the FSM easier
278:            word_size_bytes = 4'd8;
279:            rd_op           = 1'b0;
280:         end
281: 
282:        `DBG_AXI_CMD_BREAD8:
283:         begin
284:            word_size_bits  = 6'd7;  // Bits is actually bits-1, to make the FSM easier
285:            word_size_bytes = 4'd1;
286:            rd_op           = 1'b1;
287:         end
288: 
289:        `DBG_AXI_CMD_BREAD16:
290:         begin
291:            word_size_bits  = 6'd15;  // Bits is actually bits-1, to make the FSM easier
292:            word_size_bytes = 4'd2;
293:            rd_op           = 1'b1;
294:         end
295:        
296:        `DBG_AXI_CMD_BREAD32:
297:         begin
298:            word_size_bits  = 6'd31;  // Bits is actually bits-1, to make the FSM easier
299:            word_size_bytes = 4'd4;
300:            rd_op           = 1'b1;
301:         end
302: 
303:        `DBG_AXI_CMD_BREAD64:
304:         begin
305:            word_size_bits  = 6'd63;  // Bits is actually bits-1, to make the FSM easier
306:            word_size_bytes = 4'd4;
307:            rd_op           = 1'b1;
308:         end
309: 
310:         default:
311:         begin
312:            word_size_bits  = 6'b00_0000;
313:            word_size_bytes = 4'b0000;
314:            rd_op           = 1'b0;
315:         end
316: 
317:         endcase
318:    end
319: 
320: 
321:    ////////////////////////////////////////////////
322:

{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "406:    //////////////////////////////////////
407:      // Bit counter
408: 
409:    always_ff @(posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
410:    begin
411:       if(~trstn_i)          bit_count <= 6'h0;
412:       else if(bit_ct_rst)   bit_count <= 6'h0;
413:       else if(bit_ct_en)    bit_count <= bit_count + 6'h1;
414:    end
415: 
416:    assign bit_count_max = (bit_count == word_size_bits) ? 1'b1 : 1'b0 ;
417:    assign bit_count_32 = (bit_count == 6'h20) ? 1'b1 : 1'b0;
418: 
419:    ////////////////////////////////////////
420:    // Word counter
421: 
422:    assign data_to_word_counter = (word_ct_sel) ?  decremented_word_count : count_data_in;
423:    assign decremented_word_count = word_count - 16'h1;
424: 
425:    // Technically, since this data (sometimes) comes from the input shift reg, we should latch on
426:    // negedge, per the JTAG spec. But that makes things difficult when incrementing.
427:    always_ff @(posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)  // JTAG spec specifies latch on negative edge in UPDATE_DR state
428:    begin
429:       if(~trstn_i)
430:          word_count <= 16'h0;
431:       else if(word_ct_en)
432:          word_count <= data_to_word_counter;
433:    end
434: 
435:    assign word_count_zero = (word_count == 16'h0);
436: 
437:    /////////////////////////////////////////////////////
438:    // Output register and TDO output MUX
439: 
440:   assign out_reg_data = (out_reg_data_sel) ? data_from_internal_reg : {1'b0,data_from_biu};
441: 
442:    always_ff @(posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
443:    begin
444:       if(~trstn_i) data_out_shift_reg <= 'h0;
445:       else if(out_reg_ld_en) data_out_shift_reg <= out_reg_data;
446:       else if(out_reg_shift_en) data_out_shift_reg <= {1'b0, data_out_shift_reg[64:1]};
447:    end
448: 
449: 
450:    always_comb // @ (tdo_output_sel or data_out_shift_reg[0] or biu_ready or crc_match or crc_serial_out)
451:    begin
452:       if(tdo_output_sel == 2'h0)
453:          module_tdo_o = biu_ready;
454:       else  if(tdo_output_sel == 2'h1)
455:                module_tdo_o = data_out_shift_reg[0];
456:             else if(tdo_output_sel == 2'h2)
457:                      module_tdo_o = crc_match;
458:                  else module_tdo_o = crc_serial_out;
459:    end
460: 
461:    ////////////////////////////////////////
462:    // Bus Interface Unit
463:    // It is assumed that the BIU has internal registers, and will
464:    // latch address, operation, and write data on rising clock edge

592:       STATE_Rbegin:
593:       begin

599: 
600:       STATE_Rready:
601:       begin
602:          if(module_select_i && capture_dr_i)
603:             module_next_state = STATE_Rstatus;
604:          else
605:             module_next_state = STATE_Rready;

607:       
608:       STATE_Rstatus:
609:       begin
610:          if(update_dr_i)
611:             module_next_state = STATE_idle;
612:          else  if (biu_ready)
613:                   module_next_state = STATE_Rburst;
614:                else
615:                   module_next_state = STATE_Rstatus;
616:       end
617: 
618:       STATE_Rburst:
619:       begin
620:            if(update_dr_i)
621:                module_next_state = STATE_idle;
622:            else  if(bit_count_max && word_count_zero)
623:                     module_next_state = STATE_Rcrc;
624:                  else
625:                     module_next_state = STATE_Rburst;
626:       end
627: 
628:       STATE_Rcrc:
629:       begin
630:            if(update_dr_i)
631:                module_next_state = STATE_idle;
632:            // This doubles as the 'recovery' state, so stay here until update_dr_i.
633:             else 
634:                module_next_state = STATE_Rcrc;
635:       end
636: 
637:       STATE_Wready:
638:       begin
639:           if(word_count_zero)
640:              module_next_state = STATE_idle;
641:           else if(module_select_i && capture_dr_i) 
642:                   module_next_state = STATE_Wwait;
643:                else
644:                   module_next_state = STATE_Wready;
645:       end
646: 
647:       STATE_Wwait:
648:       begin
649:            if(update_dr_i)
650:                module_next_state = STATE_idle;  // client terminated early
651:            else   if (module_select_i && data_register_i[63])
652:                      module_next_state = STATE_Wburst; // Got a start bit
653:                   else
654:                      module_next_state = STATE_Wwait;

656:

659:            if(update_dr_i)

661:                else  if(bit_count_max)
662:                      begin
663:                         if(word_count_zero)
664:                            module_next_state = STATE_Wcrc;
665:                         else
666:                            module_next_state = STATE_Wburst;
667:                      end
668:                      else 
669:                         module_next_state = STATE_Wburst;
670:       end
671: 
672:       STATE_Wstatus:
673:       begin
674:            if(update_dr_i)
675:                module_next_state = STATE_idle;  // client terminated early    
676:            else if(word_count_zero)
677:                   module_next_state = STATE_Wcrc;
678:            // can't wait until bus ready if multiple devices in chain...
679:            // Would have to read postfix_bits, then send another start bit and push it through
680:            // prefix_bits...potentially very inefficient.
681:                 else

683:       end
684: 
685:       STATE_Wcrc:

687:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state = STATE_idle;  // client terminated early
688:            else if(bit_count_32) module_next_state = STATE_Wmatch;
689:            else module_next_state = STATE_Wcrc;
690:       end
691: 
692:       STATE_Wmatch:
693:       begin
694:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state = STATE_idle;
695:            // 
[TRUNCATED]

776:       STATE_Rstatus:
777:       begin
778:            tdo_output_sel  = 2'h0;
779:            top_inhibit_o   = 1'b1;    // in case of early termination
780: 
781:            if (module_next_state == STATE_Rburst)
782:            begin
783:              error_reg_en     = 1'b1;       // Check the wb_error bit
784:              out_reg_data_sel = 1'b0;  // select BIU data
785:              out_reg_ld_en    = 1'b1;
786:              bit_ct_rst       = 1'b1;
787:              word_ct_sel      = 1'b1;
788:              word_ct_en       = 1'b1;
789:              if(!(decremented_word_count == 0) && !word_count_zero)
790:              begin  // Start a biu read transaction
791:                 biu_strobe    = 1'b1;
792:                 addr_sel      = 1'b1;
793:                 addr_ct_en    = 1'b1;
794:              end
795:            end
796:       end
797: 
798:       STATE_Rburst:
799:       begin
800:         tdo_output_sel     = 2'h1;
801:         out_reg_shift_en   = 1'b1;
802:         bit_ct_en          = 1'b1;
803:         crc_en             = 1'b1;
804:         crc_in_sel         = 1'b0;  // read data in output shift register LSB (tdo)
805:         top_inhibit_o      = 1'b1;  // in case of early termination
806: 
807:         if(bit_count_max)
808:         begin
809:           error_reg_en     = 1'b1;       // Check the wb_error bit
810:           out_reg_data_sel = 1'b0;  // select BIU data
811:           out_reg_ld_en    = 1'b1;
812:           bit_ct_rst       = 1'b1;
813:           word_ct_sel      = 1'b1;
814:           word_ct_en       = 1'b1;
815:           if(!(decremented_word_count == 0) && !word_count_zero)  // Start a biu read transaction
816:           begin
817:             biu_strobe     = 1'b1;
818:             addr_sel       = 1'b1;
819:             addr_ct_en     = 1'b1;
820:           end
821:         end
822:       end
823: 
824:       STATE_Rcrc:
825:       begin
826:          // Just shift out the data, don't bother counting, we don't move on until update_dr_i
827:          tdo_output_sel = 2'h3;
828:          crc_shift_en   = 1'b1;
829:          top_inhibit_o  = 1'b1;
830:       end
831: 
832:       STATE_Wready:
833:       ; // Just a wait state
834: 
835:       STATE_Wwait:
836:       begin
837:          tdo_output_sel = 2'h1;
838:          top_inhibit_o  = 1'b1;    // in case of early termination
839:          if(module_next_state == STATE_Wburst)
840:          begin
841:             biu_clr_err    = 1'b1;  // If error occurred on last transaction of last burst, biu_err is still set.  Clear it.
842:             bit_ct_en      = 1'b1;
843:             word_ct_sel    = 1'b1;  // Pre-decrement the byte count
844:             word_ct_en     = 1'b1;
845:             crc_en         = 1'b1;  // CRC gets tdi_i, which is 1 cycle ahead of data_register_i, so we need the bit there now in the CRC
846:             crc_in_sel     = 1'b1;  // read data from tdi_i
847:          end
848:       end
849: 
850:       STATE_Wburst:
851:       begin
852:          bit_ct_en       = 1'b1;
853:          tdo_output_sel  = 2'h1;
854:          crc_en          = 1'b1;
855:          crc_in_sel      = 1'b1;  // read data from tdi_i
856:          top_inhibit_o   = 1'b1;    // in case of early termination
857: 
858:          // It would be better to do this in STATE_Wstatus, but we don't use that state 
859:          // if ADBG_USE_HISPEED is defined.  
860:          if(bit_count_max)
861:          begin
862:             error_reg_en = 1'b1;       // Check the wb_error bit
863:             bit_ct_rst   = 1'b1;  // Zero the bit count
864:             // start transaction. Can't do this here if not hispeed, biu_ready
865:             // is the status bit, and it's 0 if we start a transaction here.
866:             biu_strobe   = 1'b1;  // Start a BIU transaction
867:             addr_ct_en   = 1'b1;  // Increment thte address counter
868:             // Also can't dec the byte count yet unless hispeed,
869:             // that

11: `include "adbg_defines.v"
12: `include "adbg_lint_defines.v"

14: // Top module
15: module adbg_lint_module
16: #(
17:     parameter ADDR_WIDTH = 32,
18:     parameter DATA_WIDTH = 64,
19:     parameter AUX_WIDTH = 6
20: )
21: (
22:         // JTAG signals
23: 		input  logic                                tck_i,
24: 		output logic                                module_tdo_o,
25: 		input  logic                                tdi_i,
26: 
27:         // TAP states
28: 		input  logic                                capture_dr_i,
29: 		input  logic                                shift_dr_i,
30: 		input  logic                                update_dr_i,
31: 
32: 		input  logic [`DBG_TOP_MODULE_DATA_LEN-1:0] data_register_i,  // the data register is at top level, shared between all modules
33: 		input  logic                                module_select_i,
34: 		output logic                                top_inhibit_o,
35: 		input  logic                                trstn_i,
36: 
37: 		input  logic                        clk_i,
38: 		input  logic                        rstn_i,
39: 
40:     output logic                        lint_req_o,
41:     output logic   [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]     lint_add_o,
42:     output logic                        lint_wen_o,
43:     output logic   [DATA_WIDTH-1:0]     lint_wdata_o,
44:     output logic [DATA_WIDTH/8-1:0]     lint_be_o,
45:     output logic    [AUX_WIDTH-1:0]     lint_aux_o,
46:     input  logic                        lint_gnt_i,
47:     input  logic                        lint_r_aux_i,
48:     input  logic                        lint_r_valid_i,
49:     input  logic   [DATA_WIDTH-1:0]     lint_r_rdata_i,
50:     input  logic                        lint_r_opc_i
51: 
52: 		);
53: 
54: 
55:    // Registers to hold state etc.
56:    reg                        [31:0] address_counter;     // Holds address for next Wishbone access
57:    reg                         [5:0] bit_count;            // How many bits have been shifted in/out
58:    reg                        [15:0] word_count;          // bytes remaining in current burst command
59:    reg                         [3:0] operation;            // holds the current command (rd/wr, word size)
60:    reg                        [64:0] data_out_shift_reg;  // 32 bits to accomodate the internal_reg_error
61:    reg [`DBG_LINT_REGSELECT_SIZE-1:0] internal_register_select;  // Holds index of currently selected register
62:    reg                        [32:0] internal_reg_error;  // WB error module internal register.  32 bit address + error bit (LSB)
63: 
64: 
65:    // Control signals for the various counters / registers / state machines
66:    reg                               addr_sel;                 // Selects data for address_counter. 0 = data_register_i, 1 = incremented address count
67:    reg                               addr_ct_en;               // Enable signal for address counter register
68:    reg                               op_reg_en;                // Enable signal for 'operation' register
69:    reg                               bit_ct_en;                // enable bit counter
70:    reg

201:            word_size_bits <= 6'd63;  // Bits is actually bits-1, to make the FSM easier
202:            word_size_bytes <= 4'd4;
203:            rd_op <= 1'b1;
204:         end
205:           default:
206:          begin
207:             word_size_bits <= 6'hXX;
208:            word_size_bytes <= 4'hX;
209:            rd_op <= 1'bX;

212:      end
213: 
214: 
215:    ////////////////////////////////////////////////
216:    // Module-internal register select register (no, that's not redundant.)
217:    // Also internal register output MUX
218: 
219:     always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
220:     begin
221:         if(~trstn_i)
222:             internal_register_select = 1'h0;
223:         else if (regsel_ld_en)
224:             internal_register_select = reg_select_data;
225:     end
226: 
227:    // This is completely unnecessary here, since the WB module has only 1 internal
228:    // register.  However, to make the module expandable, it is included anyway.
229:    always @ (internal_register_select or internal_reg_error)
230:      begin
231:     case(internal_register_select)
232:           `DBG_LINT_INTREG_ERROR: data_from_internal_reg = internal_reg_error;
233:           default: data_from_internal_reg = internal_reg_error;
234:     endcase
235:      end
236: 
237: 
238: 
239:    ////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
240:    // Module-internal registers
241:    // These have generic read/write/select code, but
242:    // individual registers may have special behavior, defined here.
243: 
244:    // This is the bus error register, which traps WB errors
245:    // We latch every new BIU address in the upper 32 bits, so we always have the address for the transaction which
246:    // generated the error (the address counter might increment, esp. for writes)
247:    // We stop latching addresses when the error bit (bit 0) is set. Keep the error bit set until it is
248:    // manually cleared by a module internal register write.
249:    // Note we use reg_select_data straight from data_register_i, rather than the latched version -
250:    // otherwise, we would write the previously selected register.
251: 
252: 
253:     always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
254:     begin
255:         if(~trstn_i)
256:             internal_reg_error = 33'h0;
257:         else if(intreg_ld_en && (reg_select_data == `DBG_LINT_INTREG_ERROR))  // do load from data input register
258:         begin
259:             if(data_register_i[46])
260:                 internal_reg_error[0] = 1'b0;  // if write data is 1, reset the error bit
261:         end
262:         else if(error_reg_en && !internal_reg_error[0])
263:         begin
264:             if(biu_err || (!biu_ready))  internal_reg_error[0] = 1'b1;
265:              else if(biu_strobe) internal_reg_error[32:1] = address_counter;
266:         end
267:         else if(biu_strobe && !internal_reg_error[0])
268:             internal_reg_error[32:1] = address_counter;  // When no error, latch this whether error_reg_en or not
269:         end
270: 
271:    ///////////////////////////////////////////////
272:    // Address counter
273: 
274:    assign data_to_addr_counter = (addr_sel) ? incremented_address[31:0] : address_data_in;
275:    assign incremented_address = address_counter + word_size_bytes;
276: 
277:    // Technically, since this data (sometimes) comes from the input shift reg, we should latch on
278:    // negedge, per the JTAG spec. But that makes things difficult when incrementing.
279:    always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)  // JTAG spec specifies latch on negative edge in UPDATE_DR state
280:      begin
281:     if(~trstn_i)
282:       address_counter <= 32'h0;
283:     else if(addr_ct_en)
284:       address_counter <= data_to_addr_counter;
285
[TRUNCATED]

431:    // Determination of next state; purely combinatorial
432:    always @ (module_state or module_select_i or module_cmd or update_dr_i or capture_dr_i or operation_in[2]
433:          or word_count_zero or bit_count_max or data_register_i[63] or bit_count_32 or biu_ready or burst_read or burst_write)
434:      begin
435:     case(module_state)
436:        STATE_idle:
437:         begin
438:            if(module_cmd && module_select_i && update_dr_i && burst_read)
439:                 module_next_state <= STATE_Rbegin;
440:            else if(module_cmd && module_select_i && update_dr_i && burst_write)
441:                 module_next_state <= STATE_Wready;
442:            else module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
443:         end
444: 
445:        STATE_Rbegin:
446:         begin
447:            if(word_count_zero) module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // set up a burst of size 0, illegal.
448:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Rready;
449:         end
450:        STATE_Rready:
451:         begin
452:            if(module_select_i && capture_dr_i) module_next_state <= STATE_Rstatus;
453:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Rready;
454:         end
455:        STATE_Rstatus:
456:         begin
457:            if(update_dr_i) module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
458:            else if (biu_ready) module_next_state <= STATE_Rburst;
459:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Rstatus;
460:         end
461:        STATE_Rburst:
462:         begin
463:            if(update_dr_i) module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
464:            else if(bit_count_max && word_count_zero) module_next_state <= STATE_Rcrc;
465:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Rburst;
466:         end
467:        STATE_Rcrc:
468:         begin
469:            if(update_dr_i) module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
470:            // This doubles as the 'recovery' state, so stay here until update_dr_i.
471:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Rcrc;
472:         end
473: 
474:        STATE_Wready:
475:         begin
476:            if(word_count_zero) module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
477:            else if(module_select_i && capture_dr_i) module_next_state <= STATE_Wwait;
478:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wready;
479:         end
480:        STATE_Wwait:

482:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // client terminated early
483:            else if(module_select_i && data_register_i[63]) module_next_state <= STATE_Wburst; // Got a start bit
484:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wwait;
485:         end
486:        STATE_Wburst:

488:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // client terminated early
489:                else if(bit_count_max)

491:             if(word_count_zero) module_next_state <= STATE_Wcrc;
492:             else module_next_state <= STATE_Wburst;
493:          end
494:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wburst;
495:         end
496:        STATE_Wstatus:
497:         begin
498:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // client terminated early
499:            else if(word_count_zero) module_next_state <= STATE_Wcrc;
500:            // can't wait until bus ready if multiple devices in chain...
501:            // Would have to read postfix_bits, then send another start bit and push it through
502:            // prefix_bits...potentially very inefficient.
503:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wburst;
504:         end
505: 
506:        STATE_Wcrc:

508:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // client terminated early
509:            else if(bit_count_32) module_next_state <= STATE_Wmatch;

module adv_dbg_if
    #(
        parameter NB_CORES       = 4,
        parameter AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 32,
        parameter AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
        parameter AXI_USER_WIDTH = 6,
        parameter AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 3
    ) (
       input  logic                    tms_pad_i,
       input  logic                    tck_pad_i,
       input  logic                    trstn_pad_i,
       input  logic                    tdi_pad_i,
       output logic                    tdo_pad_o,
       output logic                    tdo_padoe_o,

       input  logic                    test_mode_i,

        // CPU signals
        input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_clk_i,
        output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] [15:0] cpu_addr_o,
        input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0] [31:0] cpu_data_i,
        output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] [31:0] cpu_data_o,
        input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_bp_i,
        output logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_stall_o,
        output logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_stb_o,
        output logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_we_o,
        input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_ack_i,
        output logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_rst_o,

		// AXI4 MASTER
		//***************************************
		input  logic                        axi_aclk,
		input  logic                        axi_aresetn,
		// WRITE ADDRESS CHANNEL
		output logic                        axi_master_aw_valid,
		output logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]   axi_master_aw_addr,
		output logic [2:0]                  axi_master_aw_prot,
		output logic [3:0]                  axi_master_aw_region,
		output logic [7:0]                  axi_master_aw_len,
		output logic [2:0]                  axi_master_aw_size,
		output logic [1:0]                  axi_master_aw_burst,
		output logic                        axi_master_aw_lock,
		output logic [3:0]                  axi_master_aw_cache,
		output logic [3:0]                  axi_master_aw_qos,
		output logic [AXI_ID_WIDTH-1:0]     axi_master_aw_id,
		output logic [AXI_USER_WIDTH-1:0]   axi_master_aw_user,
		input  logic                        axi_master_aw_ready,

		// READ ADDRESS CHANNEL
		output logic                        axi_master_ar_valid,
		output logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]   axi_master_ar_addr,
		output logic [2:0]                  axi_master_ar_prot,
		output logic [3:0]                  axi_master_ar_region,
		output logic [7:0]                  axi_master_ar_len,
		output logic [2:0]                  axi_master_ar_size,
		output logic [1:0]                  axi_master_ar_b
